frameworks.name: default-mode network
frameworks.one_line: Brain network deactivated during cognitive load tasks, supporting social cognition.

frameworks.name: dual-process theory
frameworks.one_line: Standard framework for cognitive load that this paper reconsiders.

frameworks.name: social cognition
frameworks.one_line: Process tied to DMN that may be disrupted under cognitive load.

core_question: Can apparent cognitive load effects be reinterpreted as disruptions of default-mode network processes rather than capacity limits?
key_assumptions: Typical cognitive load tasks deactivate the DMN
DMN supports processes including social cognition
Standard cognitive load interpretations rest on dual-process theory
key_findings: Typical cognitive load tasks deactivate the default-mode network
Past cognitive load studies are reinterpreted through a DMN lens
interpretations: Apparent cognitive load effects may arise from disruption of DMN processes including social cognition
Dual-process theory of cognitive load should be reconsidered
